mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and improves risk appetite, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when policy easing is broad enough to reduce financing pressure rather than reflect an acute recession scare.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising policy rates, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is decelerating and FEDFUNDS stays elevated, real policy tightness eases and equity duration risk falls, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when inflation is reaccelerating because high nominal rates then signal restrictive policy rather than relief.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below its prior 12-month average while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show higher median 60-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is below 2% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income expectations, which depresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when unemployment rises quickly rather than drifting sideways.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are not negative in a majority of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration beyond seasonal noise, which lowers earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days. The signal is not decomposable into either series alone because the joint pattern identifies real hiring weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform single-variable signals, or if triggered 60-day SPX returns are positive more often than negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising RSAFS series reflects stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should weaken when retail sales are flat or contracting because revenue growth then fails to confirm demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth does not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST above its recent trend signals firm residential demand and better cyclical growth expectations, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when housing starts rise only because of post-shock volatility without a concurrent improvement in activity.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-strength episodes do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the signal flips negative in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL inflation is cooling, supply capacity is improving faster than cost pressure, which supports margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism breaks if output growth is accompanied by accelerating inflation because margins then face cost pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up/CPI-down condition fails to outperform INDPRO alone, or if median 20-day SPX returns are not positive in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening or post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in GDPC1 following prior weakness predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings expectations recover with real activity. The signal should not be used outside recovery phases, where quarterly GDP rebounds can be backward-looking and stale.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebounds do not lead to higher 60-day SPX returns during recovery windows, or if the signal is positive in fewer than 55% of recovery-regime observations.
